2) Please ensure authz_decision + authz_policy_version are present not just on “deny” outcomes, but on allowed actions too.

Connor — yes, please add the prod validation step to the release checklist. If it helps, security can provide a sample Splunk query once the event_name and schema_version are final.

Once PR is merged, tag me on the doc update for a quick review. After that, consider security unblocked for enabling the feature flag from an audit-logging perspective.

— Aisha
Dr. Aisha Rahman
CISO, Redwood Inference

title: PRD review request: Workload-aware batching defaults (fast / standard / cost-efficient tiers)
preview:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Jan 10, 2026 at 9:18 AM PT
Subject: PRD review request: Workload-aware batching defaults (fast / standard / cost-efficient tiers)

Hi Nikhil + Lauren — looping you in for PRD review. Ava FYI since this is part of the runtime cost optimization / 1.22 train.

Draft PRD (Confluence):
https://redwoodinference.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/PROD/pages/184221329/PRD+Batching+profiles+SLO+tiers+(Draft)

Linear tracking ticket:
PM-612 (https://linear.app/redwood/issue/PM-612/workload-aware-batching-defaults-prd)

What I’m looking for from you both (by Tue 1/14 if possible):

1) Tier semantics / customer-facing behavior
- Is the tier selected at route policy level only, or do we allow a request-level override (header/SDK param) for Hosted?
- For Hosted: if customer does nothing, do we default to “standard” or do we recommend “fast” for streaming chat routes?
- Any red flags on naming (fast/standard/cost-efficient) given customer expectations?

2) SLO targets and how we communicate them
- Are we comfortable publishing tier targets as guidance (p95/p99) vs only internal guardrails?
- For streaming: should we define separate targets for time-to-first-token vs steady-state token rate, or keep it simplified?

3) Overrides & precedence (Dedicated)
- Dedicated needs a clear override story. Proposed precedence in PRD is:
  customer override > route policy > tier default > runtime fallback.
  Please sanity check based on your roadmap / expected support load.

4) Rollout / kill switch expectations
- Hosted staged rollout: internal canary → 5% of orgs (opt-out) → 25% → 100%.
- Dedicated: explicit opt-in first.
- I’m proposing a “kill switch” that reverts to existing manual batching defaults if any tier blows p99 gates for >15m in a region.

5) Observability / “why this profile”
- Need agreement on what we show to customers in Console vs what stays internal.
- Proposed in PRD: tier label + reason codes (default/override/model-constraint/overload-mode), but no raw queue depth exposed by default.

Specific questions I’m stuck on (and would love your POV):
- Should we treat streaming endpoints as “fast” by default (seems right for support-agent/chat) OR keep standard as default to avoid surprising throughput deltas and let customers choose fast explicitly?
- How strict should we be about allowing a per-request tier selection in Hosted? (pro: experimentation; con: support/debug complexity + higher cardinality in metrics)

Thanks — please comment directly in Confluence if easier. If you’d rather do a 20 min sync, I can make time Monday afternoon.

Aditya

—
Aditya Rao
PM, Redwood Optimize
Redwood Inference


On Fri, Jan 10, 2026 at 1:42 PM Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Jan 10, 2026 at 1:42 PM PT
Subject: Re: PRD review request: Workload-aware batching defaults (fast / standard / cost-efficient tiers)

Reviewed and left comments in the PRD (mostly in the “Hosted API behavior” section).

My takes (summary here):

- Default tier for Hosted if unspecified: I strongly prefer STANDARD across the board.
  Rationale: we already have streaming routes with customer-provided prompt distributions; if we default to FAST for “streaming=true” we’ll get a support wave when customers see cost/throughput shift (even if latency improves). Better to keep predictable and message FAST as recommended for “realtime/chat UX”.

- Request-level override (header/SDK param): OK to have, but I’d frame it as “experimental” and/or feature-flagged initially.
  Concern is operational: a lot of customers will try to do per-request fast/standard toggles and then file tickets when they can’t explain tail behavior. Also metric cardinality risk if we let arbitrary tier tagging appear on every route.

- How to talk about targets: I’d avoid publishing hard p99 commitments in public docs initially. I’m fine with “expected behavior” guidance and keeping the strict gates internal (esp. early rollout).

- Naming: fast/standard/cost-efficient is fine. Avoid “realtime” (customers interpret as hard guarantee). Also please add one sentence in docs/Console copy that cost-efficient may add queueing under burst.

If helpful I can provide suggested copy for the “What happens if I don’t pick a tier?” FAQ.

Nikhil

—
Nikhil Sharma
Product, Hosted API
Redwood Inference


On Fri, Jan 10, 2026 at 2:28 PM Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Jan 10, 2026 at 2:28 PM PT
Subject: Re: PRD review request: Workload-aware batching defaults (fast / standard / cost-efficient tiers)

+1 to Nikhil’s point on defaulting to STANDARD.

Dedicated-specific notes (also added inline comments):

- Precedence: customer override > route policy > tier default makes sense, but we need to define what “customer override” means concretely.
  For Dedicated, customers will expect:
  (a) per-tenant override (applies to all routes for that tenant), and
  (b) route-level override (applies only to a specific endpoint).
  If we support both, we should state the order explicitly (route override should win over tenant default).

- SLAs: if a Dedicated customer selects FAST, we should be careful not to imply the platform will “meet fast SLO” regardless of their capacity sizing.
  Request: add a sentence that tier behavior is bounded by provisioned capacity, and mention recommended sizing guidance lives in Dedicated onboarding docs.

- Rollout: opt-in first is required. Also I’d like the kill switch to be per-cluster (not global) so we don’t penalize healthy regions/clusters.

- Supportability: please ensure the “effective profile” view includes whether a customer is pinned to a tier or using default, and whether any overload mode was entered. That’s the first question support will ask.

Happy to sync with you + the runtime folks next week if needed.

Lauren

—
Lauren Bishop
Product, Redwood Dedicated
Redwood Inference


On Fri, Jan 10, 2026 at 4:37 PM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Jan 10, 2026 at 4:37 PM PT
Subject: Re: PRD review request: Workload-aware batching defaults (fast / standard / cost-efficient tiers)

Thanks for driving this, Aditya.

Two asks to incorporate before we socialize broadly:

1) Go/No-Go criteria: please make the launch gates unambiguous.
- What metrics must be green (by tier) and for how long?
- What is the rollback trigger vs “investigate but continue canary” trigger?

2) Customer promise vs internal gates: align with Nikhil. Keep the public messaging focused on trade-offs and recommended usage. We can publish tighter targets later once we have stable baselines across top models/regions.

If you update the PRD with those items, I’m comfortable using it for the next exec staff checkpoint.

Ava

—
Ava Chen
VP Product
Redwood Inference

title: Request: Consolidated subprocessor rollup + evidence checklist
preview:
From: Oliver Park <opssec@novamed.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@novamed.com>
Date: Mon, 08 Dec 2025 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Consolidated subprocessor rollup and attestation request

Hi Avery,

Thanks for the conversation last week. As part of our annual vendor assurance cycle we need a consolidated subprocessor rollup (snapshot) and an evidence checklist aligned to SOC 2 and SIG-Lite. Specifically NovaMed needs: 

- Current subprocessor roster with roles and residency (onshore/offshore)
- Mapping of subprocessor functions to SOC 2 controls (if possible)
- Copies or summary of recent SOC 2 evidence/attestation (cover letter or summary PDF is fine)
- SIG-Lite answers for hosting, KMS/key residency, and telemetry/retention

Can you confirm whether Redwood can provide a rolling snapshot (i.e., a CSV with timestamps for each subprocessor) and a single ZIP with the attestation material by EOD Wednesday (12/10)?

If any of this needs a different cadence or routing to your compliance team, let me know and loop them in.

Best,
Oliver
Ops Security Lead
NovaMed

--
attachment-stub: requirements_novamed_2025.pdf (pdf)

From: Avery Johnson <avery@redwoodinference.com>
To: Oliver Park <opssec@novamed.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 08 Dec 2025 10:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Consolidated subprocessor rollup and attestation request

Oliver —

Appreciate the clear ask. We can produce a rolling snapshot CSV plus a bundled evidence package. Quick clarifiers to make sure we meet NovaMed's requirements: 

1) For "mapping of subprocessor functions to SOC 2 controls," do you want a high-level control map (control IDs -> vendor function) or a detailed artifact pointer (e.g., specific evidence file names)?
2) KMS residency: do you need table-level residency (which provider holds keys for which subprocessor) or just "region" (US/EU/APAC)?
3) Are there any specific classifications (PHI/PPI) in scope that require additional redaction?

We will aim for EOD 12/10 but may deliver the CSV first and the larger ZIP shortly after if that works for you. Adding Marissa and Ben from our compliance and vendor teams to assist with evidence packaging.

Thanks,
Avery
Senior Account Lead — Redwood Inference

--

title: Exec approval: Enterprise priority semantics + hot-route protection rollout (Hosted API)
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Dec 6, 2025 at 9:14 AM PST
Subject: Exec approval: Enterprise priority semantics + hot-route protection rollout (Hosted API)
Attachments: Customer_Comms_Draft_Priority_Routing_Quotas_v3.docx; HotRoute_Protection_Rollout_Risk_Assessment_v2.pdf

Neha,

Requesting exec approval to proceed with the enterprise-priority semantics for Hot-route Capacity Protection and to authorize the staged rollout plan (us-east canary -> multi-region).

Decision needed:
1) Customer-facing semantics: We will explicitly treat enterprise traffic on protected routes (/v1/chat/completions, /v1/embeddings, /v1/rerank) as “priority class” with reserved per-route/per-tier budgets.
2) Overload behavior: Under pressure, best-effort/self-serve traffic will be throttled first (429) and may be shed earlier; enterprise should preserve SLOs until enterprise budgets are exhausted.
3) Messaging: We plan to communicate as “capacity protection to maintain stability” (not “paid fast lane”), with clear 429 reason codes + retry guidance.

Risk assessment summary (full detail in attached PDF):
- Risk: 429 spikes trigger customer confusion/escalations (esp. during rollout). Mitigation: consistent 429 payload/headers + support macros; staged allowlist; canary guardrails.
- Risk: “silent” quality changes if fallback to alt model/region. Mitigation: for enterprise routes, fallback is opt-in and limited by route guardrails; otherwise strict admission control only.
- Risk: added data plane latency from policy evaluation. Mitigation: cached/batched lookups; SLA budgeted overhead <1ms p95 at gateway.
- Risk: alerting noise/paging storms. Mitigation: burn-rate + sustained trigger alerts; no paging on expected best-effort shedding.

Rollout plan:
- Dec 10–12: us-east canary (allowlisted enterprise accounts only), 1% -> 10% -> 50% traffic on protected routes.
- Week of Dec 15: us-west + eu-west, expand allowlist.
- GA target: end of Dec, contingent on canary success metrics.

Success criteria (canary):
- Enterprise protected routes: no regression in p95 latency; reduced 5xx during induced load tests; observed admission control activation w/ stable enterprise.
- Best-effort: 429 is predictable w/ correct reason codes; no gateway error spikes.

Asks:
- Can you approve the semantics + rollout plan as outlined?
- Any redlines on the customer comms framing (attached doc)?

Thanks,
Ava

—
Ava Chen
Redwood Inference



From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Dec 6, 2025 at 11:02 AM PST
Subject: Re: Exec approval: Enterprise priority semantics + hot-route protection rollout (Hosted API)

Thanks Ava.

Before I sign off, I need clarity on two points:

1) “Enterprise budgets exhausted” — what happens in that state? Is it 429 with a distinct reason code vs 503? And are we committing to any “never throttle enterprise” language in comms?

2) Any scenario where best-effort shedding could cascade into enterprise impact (shared GPU pools, head-of-line blocking)? If yes, what’s the explicit backpressure policy and what signals trigger it?

Also, please confirm Support readiness: do we have an escalation path + exception process documented for burst-limit asks?

-Neha



From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sat, Dec 6, 2025 at 1:27 PM PST
Subject: Re: Exec approval: Enterprise priority semantics + hot-route protection rollout (Hosted API)
Attachments: Launch_Checklist_Hot_Route_Protection_US_East_Canary.xlsx

Neha,

Answers inline:

1) Enterprise budgets exhausted behavior
- Response: 429 (not 503) for admission-control denies, with consistent schema + headers.
- Reason codes:
  - quota_exceeded (standard)
  - rate_limited (standard)
  - overload_protection (new; means fleet/route pressure)
  - enterprise_route_budget_exhausted (new; indicates the enterprise-reserved budget is depleted)
- Comms: we are not using “never throttle enterprise” anywhere. Draft language: “Enterprise tiers are prioritized to maintain stability during fleet pressure; requests may still be rate limited if contractual or configured limits are exceeded.”

2) Preventing best-effort -> enterprise cascade
- The main failure mode we’re addressing is best-effort bursts driving queue depth/scheduler lag so high that enterprise tail latency and 5xx increase.
- Policy: gate best-effort earlier using capacity signals (queue depth + scheduler lag) + per-route admission control. Once triggers trip, best-effort is throttled and/or shed; enterprise continues to be admitted until enterprise budget or hard safety thresholds are hit.
- Signals/triggers: we’re standardizing on queue depth + scheduler lag over raw GPU utilization (less noisy). Trigger is sustained threshold over N seconds (avoids flapping). On trigger, routing moves best-effort to “deny unless under baseline” and preserves enterprise budgets.

Support readiness + exceptions
- Yes: Support has an escalation path for burst-limit asks. The planned workflow is: Support -> TAM/CS -> Solutions -> Platform on-call for temp exception (time-bounded + guardrails) with logging.
- We’re not changing billing; exceptions are policy config only.

I attached the canary launch checklist (owner names + go/no-go gates). If you want, I can add a specific “enterprise budget exhausted” comms snippet to the draft for clarity.

-Ava



From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Dec 7, 2025 at 8:41 AM PST
Subject: Re: Exec approval: Enterprise priority semantics + hot-route protection rollout (Hosted API)

This is directionally good.

Two conditions for approval:

A) Comms must avoid the term “priority class” externally. Use “capacity protection” / “stability protections” language. Internally we can call it priority.

B) Add an explicit internal-only note that if enterprise hard safety thresholds trip (e.g., queue depth beyond safe operation), we may deny enterprise with 429 and “overload_protection” and we should page SRE immediately. I want that to be part of the operational contract.

If you can confirm those are addressed, I’m comfortable approving the us-east allowlisted canary.

-Neha



From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, Dec 7, 2025 at 2:16 PM PST
Subject: Re: Exec approval: Enterprise priority semantics + hot-route protection rollout (Hosted API)

Confirmed on both conditions.

A) Customer comms: we’ll remove/avoid “priority class” terminology. We’ll position as “stability protections for enterprise routes” and focus on predictable 429 behavior + retry guidance.

B) Operational contract: agreed. We’ll document that if hard safety thresholds trip, enterprise can be denied with 429 + overload_protection, and that condition triggers paging + incident-bot notification. (We’ll keep “enterprise_route_budget_exhausted” reserved for normal budget depletion; hard safety is a separate overload path.)

I’ll update the comms draft + checklist today and send you the final versions.

-Ava



From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, Dec 8, 2025 at 4:02 PM PST
Subject: Re: Exec approval: Enterprise priority semantics + hot-route protection rollout (Hosted API)

Approved for the us-east allowlisted canary under the conditions we discussed.

Please make sure we have:
- clear rollback criteria (latency regression, 5xx increase, unexpected enterprise denies)
- Support/CS briefed before the first enablement window
- a short post-canary readout (24h + 72h) with enterprise SLO impact + shed metrics

Thanks,
Neha

—
Neha Kapoor
Exec Sponsor (Reliability)
Redwood Inference

title: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)
preview:
From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 9:12 AM PST
Subject: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

Sean — requesting SRE + exec approval to enable the mixed-workload scheduler prototype behind flag in a *limited* production canary (Hosted API, us-east-1).

Why now
- We have repeat internal + customer escalations where mixed short/long prompts inflate p95/TTFT (HOL blocking during long prefill).
- The runtime team has a prototype (two-queue short/long + aging + token budget guardrails) that shows consistent p95 improvement on mixed distributions without material throughput loss in our benchmark suite.

What we’re proposing (scope + blast radius)
- Region/pool: Hosted API us-east-1, pool: hosted-canary (not general).
- Traffic: 1% -> 5% (same pool) over ~3 hours if metrics stay within guardrails.
- Duration: 24 hours at 5% (or revert sooner if drift).
- Feature gating:
  - Flag: scheduler.mixed_workload.enabled=true (pool-scoped)
  - Policy knobs pinned (no auto-tuning) for the canary window.

Success criteria (must hit)
- Mixed workload p95 latency (end-to-end) improves >= 15% vs baseline (comparable traffic band), measured on canary + perf-canary.
- No p95 regression > 5% on steady-state short-only workload.
- No error-rate increase beyond normal variance; no sustained queue starvation counters.

Guardrails / rollback triggers (aligned to SRE doc)
- Rollback immediately if any of:
  - p95 (Hosted API chat) +10% for > 10 minutes vs same-hour baseline
  - p99 +15% for > 10 minutes
  - starvation_long_counter increments continuously for > 2 minutes (indicates long queue not making progress)
  - elevated OOM/compute errors attributable to batch construction changes
- Rollback options:
  1) Flip flag off (fast, no deploy) — expected to drain within minutes.
  2) Revert knobs to baseline (if only parameter-induced).
  3) Runtime rollback to 1.17.3 in hosted-canary (Connor has the release playbook ready).

Known risks (and mitigations)
- Risk: long-queue starvation under sustained short traffic
  - Mitigation: aging + min dispatch share + regression test merged (PR #24905).
- Risk: kernel selection instability when alternating short/long sequences
  - Mitigation: prefill/decode separation hooks + capped token budget per iteration; monitored via kernel_thrash metric + Nsight spot checks in staging.
- Risk: throughput dip due to policy overhead
  - Mitigation: overhead measured <1% CPU in staging; monitor tokens/sec and batch utilization.

Links / artifacts
- Results summary (attached): mixed-workload-results-summary-runtime-1.18.pdf
- Canary plan draft (attached): canary-plan-mixed-scheduler-us-east-v3.docx
- Confluence design + ADR:
  - https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/SERVE/Mixed-workload+scheduler+prototype+-+Design
  - https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ARCH/ADR-Short-Long-Prompt-Scheduling-Policy
- Perf-canary dashboard:
  - https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/perf-mixed/mixed-workload-perf-canary?var-runtime=1.18.0-rc3

Request
- Sean: please reply with SRE sign-off (or required edits) so Connor can schedule the window today.
- If approved, we’ll do the ramp during US business hours with Rafael as ops point and Aisha watching SLO + rollback triggers.

Thanks,
Mateo
Executive Sponsor, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference

Attachment: mixed-workload-results-summary-runtime-1.18.pdf (application/pdf)
Attachment: canary-plan-mixed-scheduler-us-east-v3.docx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document)

---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 10:03 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

A couple asks before I sign off:

1) Can you confirm the change is *pool-scoped only* (no spillover to other hosted pools via shared config)?
2) What’s our exact dashboard + alert set for queue starvation + per-class waiting time? I don’t want this to be “we’ll eyeball it.”
3) In the results summary: what’s the worst-case scenario you saw (any regressions on long-only or long-spike mixes)?

If we can nail those three, I’m comfortable with a 1% -> 5% ramp with a tight rollback.

-Sean

---

From: Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 10:36 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

Responding inline:

> 1) confirm pool-scoped only
Yes. The flag + knobs are resolved at pool config level (runtime config service), and we’re only enabling for pool=hosted-canary in us-east-1. No global default change. (Config diff is attached in the canary plan doc; Rafael can also paste the exact key path if helpful.)

> 2) dashboard + alert set
Allison has the dashboard spec + alerts wired. We’ll be monitoring:
- scheduler_queue_depth{class=short|long}
- scheduler_time_in_queue_ms_p95{class=short|long}
- scheduler_starvation_events_total{class=long}
- scheduler_dispatch_share{class=short|long}
- plus end-to-end p95/p99 and TTFT.

> 3) worst-case scenario seen
In “long-spike” synthetic (10 minutes of 70% long-context), we saw:
- Mixed p95 improved still (less HOL for short),
- Long-class p95 increased ~3–6% depending on SKU, but no starvation due to aging + min-share.
We did see one starvation corner case early (continuous short flood); that’s what PR #24905 addresses, and it’s in the 1.18.0-rc3 build proposed.

Relevant PRs:
- Two-queue policy: https://github.com/redwoodinference/redwood/pull/24844
- Metrics: https://github.com/redwoodinference/redwood/pull/24888
- Starvation fix: https://github.com/redwoodinference/redwood/pull/24905

-Lena

---

From: Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>, Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 11:05 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

Dashboard is live for hosted-canary / us-east-1:
https://grafana.redwoodinference.com/d/sched-class/scheduler-class-metrics?var-region=us-east-1&var-pool=hosted-canary

Alerts (PagerDuty: Runtime-SRE)
- “SchedulerLongClassStarvation”
  - fires if scheduler_starvation_events_total{class="long"} increases AND scheduler_time_in_queue_ms_p95{class="long"} > 20000ms for 2m
- “SchedulerClassSkewTooHigh”
  - fires if dispatch_share{class="short"} > 0.95 for 5m (indicates long not getting scheduled)
- “SchedulerQueueTimeRegressionShort”
  - fires if time_in_queue p95 short > baseline + 15% for 10m

These are documented in the dashboard “Readme” panel and mirror the Hosted API tail latency guardrails doc.

-Allison

---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 11:42 AM PST
Subject: Re: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

Approved from SRE side with the following conditions:
- Ramp stays within the guardrails described (1% -> 5% only; no additional pools without a follow-up).
- Aisha or Rafael is explicitly watching during the ramp window; if alerts fire, rollback is immediate (no “let’s wait it out”).
- Connor to confirm rollback path (flag off + runtime rollback) is ready before we start.

Thanks for putting the monitoring in place.

-Sean

---

From: Mateo Alvarez <mateo@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sofia Ivanova <sofia@redwoodinference.com>, Lena Fischer <lena@redwoodinference.com>, Rafael Mendes <rafael@redwoodinference.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor@redwoodinference.com>, Allison Grant <allison@redwoodinference.com>, Aisha Bello <aisha@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Dec 3, 2025 at 12:06 PM PST
Subject: Re: Approval request: enable mixed-workload scheduler prototype in limited prod canary (us-east)

Confirmed — we’ll follow those conditions.

Connor: please schedule the ramp for this afternoon (target start 2:00pm PT / 5:00pm ET) and reply-all with the exact change window + rollback checklist.

Rafael/Aisha: you’re on point for the ramp. If any of the three rollback triggers hit (p95/p99 drift, starvation, error-rate anomalies), roll back immediately and we’ll do the postmortem tomorrow.

Thanks all.

-Mateo

- Risk: kernel selection instability when alternating short/long sequences
  - Mitigation: prefill/decode separation hooks + capped token budget per iteration; monitored via kernel_thrash metric + Nsight spot checks in staging.
- Risk: throughput dip due to policy overhead
  - Mitigation: overhead measured <1% CPU in staging; monitor tokens/sec and batch utilization.

- Results summary (attached): mixed-workload-results-summary-runtime-1.18.pdf

- If approved, we’ll do the ramp during US business hours with Rafael as ops point and Aisha watching SLO + rollback triggers.

Thanks,
Mateo

title: Bucketed commit & geo hedging — APAC/EU/US usage mix
preview:
From: Nora Lin <nora@finapps.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Martin Cho <mcho@finapps.com>
Date: Sun, 30 Nov 2025 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Quick Q on per-token tiers + geo mix

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the product walkthrough earlier. We ran some numbers and want to understand how Redwood's hosted API pricing would work for a mixed-geo deployment. A few specific asks: 

- We expect ~1.2B tokens/mo at launch, split roughly 60% US, 30% EU, 10% APAC. How do commit tiers and per-token discounts apply when usage is cross-region?
- Do credits apply uniformly or are APAC tokens charged a surcharge?
- We need clarity on billing currency (USD vs local), VAT handling for EU, and whether data residency options change pricing.

I've attached a CSV with our projected usage by endpoint (FinApps_usage_profile.csv). Can you send a short mapping of tier thresholds and an example invoice calc?

Goal: get a 1-page commercial summary + a sample SOW we can share with procurement.

Cheers,
Nora
Nora Lin
Head of Platform, FinApps


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Nora Lin <nora@finapps.com>
Cc: Martin Cho <mcho@finapps.com>
Date: Mon, 01 Dec 2025 10:04:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Quick Q on per-token tiers + geo mix

Hi Nora — thanks for the clear note and the usage CSV. Attaching a provisional pricing matrix (redwood_pricing_matrix_Q4.xlsx) that shows examples; I'll summarize the key points inline so procurement has quick answers.

Key proposal (hosted API, usage-based with committed discounts):
- Base list rate: $0.0008 per token (baseline, US).
- Commit tiers (blended monthly effective price after commit credit):
  * 0 - 250M tokens: list price
  * 250M - 1B tokens: 12% discount off list
  * 1B - 3B tokens: 20% discount off list
  * 3B+ tokens: 30% discount off list (negotiated for multi-quarter commits)

Geo treatment and blended pricing suggestion:
- We support a single committed pool with region-weighted conversion for billing. Example: convert actual usage into "billing tokens" using region multipliers to account for local infra costs. Recommended multipliers (example): US x1.0, EU x1.03, APAC x1.10.
- Contract can lock in a blended effective price calculated as: (sum(region_multiplier * tokens_used) across regions) / committed_tokens. That gives you predictable unit economics without separate per-region commits.

Credits and surcharges:
- Committed credits are applied to the billing-token total. Any overage billed at the post-commit tiered list rate for that month.
- APAC surcharge is modeled via the multiplier above; we do not add a separate line-item surcharge if you accept the blended conversion model.

VAT and currency:
- Invoices issued in USD by default; we can issue EUR invoices for EU legal entities (exchange/FX may apply).
- VAT is applied per tax law; our billing team will provide EU-facing invoices with VAT lines where required.

Data residency and options:
- If you require EU-only data residency, we can route EU traffic to our EU clusters; that adds a 2% uplift on the blended price unless you commit to a Dedicated region (then we can discuss absorbing that uplift).

Next steps I recommend:
1) If that approach sounds ok, we'll produce a 1-page commercial term sheet and SOW outline.
2) I can loop in Evelyn (billing) for invoice templates and Jordan (sales lead) for any contractual guardrails.

Attaching the matrix and a draft SOW outline (SOW_outline_v1.pdf). Happy to jump on a 30-min call — how does Tue 11/2 or Wed 11/3 look on your side?

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Attachments: redwood_pricing_matrix_Q4.xlsx, SOW_outline_v1.pdf

On Sun, Nov 30, 2025 at 09:12 AM Nora Lin <nora@finapps.com> wrote:
> Hi Ben,
>
> Thanks for the product walkthrough earlier. We ran some numbers and want to understand how Redwood's hosted API pricing would work for a mixed-geo deployment. A few specific asks: ...


From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Nora Lin <nora@finapps.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 01 Dec 2025 15:55:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Quick Q on per-token tiers + geo mix (internal suggestion + clarifications)

Nora / Ben — quick clarifications from a product and ops POV. Adding here so procurement sees practical guardrails.

- The region multipliers Ben suggested are conservative; APAC at x1.10 covers current spot GPU and cross-region bandwidth. For larger sustained Commit (>=3B/mo), we can reduce APAC multiplier to x1.05 or remove it entirely if you take a Dedicated APAC pool.
- We recommend a quarterly geo-rebalancing clause: on a 90-day cadence we compute actual usage and allow a one-time reallocation of up to 15% of the committed pool between geographic buckets (conversion formula: billing_tokens = tokens * multiplier). This protects both parties against regional traffic shift.
- For EU VAT and data-residency: Redwood can provide EU-invoiceable contracts and guarantees that PII stays in-region when EU-only routing is selected. If that is required, note the small data residency uplift Ben mentioned (2%) unless Dedicated is chosen.
- Operational note: if you anticipate bursty APAC traffic (e.g., marketing spikes), suggest adding 25% burst headroom on the committed pool or enabling short-term burst credits tied to prepaid tokens — cheaper than constant overspend.

I'll attach the pricing matrix we just referenced. If FinApps is comfortable with the blended conversion model, legal can draft the geo-bucket rebalancing clause quickly.

Jordan Blake
Head of Sales, Redwood Inference
Attachments: redwood_pricing_matrix_Q4.xlsx

On Mon, Dec 01, 2025 at 10:04 AM Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Nora — thanks for the clear note and the usage CSV. Attaching a provisional pricing matrix (redwood_pricing_matrix_Q4.xlsx) that shows examples; I'll summarize the key points inline so procurement has quick answers.


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Nora Lin <nora@finapps.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn@redwood.com>, Martin Cho <mcho@finapps.com>
Date: Tue, 02 Dec 2025 16:03:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Bucketed commit & geo hedging — APAC/EU/US usage mix

Nora — great conversation yesterday. To keep momentum, summary & next steps: 

Summary:
- We propose a 12-month committed pool at 1.2B tokens/mo (RDW-4582) with the blended conversion multipliers: US x1.0, EU x1.03, APAC x1.10. That maps to an effective blended price of $0.00064/token at the 1B-3B tier after the 20% commit discount (example calc included in attached redlines document).
- Quarterly geo-bucket rebalancing (up to 15% shift) and a 30-day overage process at the next-tier list rate.
- If FinApps requires EU-only routing for production, we can offer a Dedicated EU pool discussion (reduce multiplier, separate SLA).

Action items:
1) Evelyn — can you prepare an EU invoice example and outline VAT treatment for FinApps legal? (cc'd)
2) Ben — Nora, I'll send a first-pass SOW with commercial terms and a redlines doc by EOD Wed 12/3.
3) Nora / Martin — please confirm whether procurement prefers USD invoicing or EUR invoicing, and whether APAC needs Dedicated capacity.

I'll attach the draft redlines (redlines_finapps.docx) for your review. If everything looks directionally right, propose a procurement call Thurs 12/4 at 11am PT to lock terms.

Thanks — Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
---
Ben Carter | ben@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0132
Redwood Inference — production-first LLM inference

title: Runway retune & risk notice — Acme MAP schedule drift
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>
To: Sara Lynn <sara.lynn@acmecorp.com>, Michael Tan <michael.tan@acmecorp.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2025-11-29T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Runway retune & risk notice — Acme MAP schedule drift

Sara / Michael,

Quick heads up — we observed a slippage in the MAP milestones after last week's integration feedback. The immediate implications: the evaluation window will slip by two business weeks unless we agree on one of the mitigation paths below. Please see the attached Acme_MAP_v2.xlsx (updated milestones) and the initial risk log.

Proposed options (pick one):
- Option A: Move evaluation start by 10 business days; Redwood provides a 2-week burst of dedicated capacity at no additional charge to keep the demo fidelity.
- Option B: Keep original dates; Acme agrees to a narrowed eval scope (core scenarios only) and accelerated feedback cadence (3x/week).
- Option C: Approve a phased acceptance — early acceptance for non-critical routes with full acceptance deferred.

Ask: can you confirm preferred option by EOD tomorrow (Mon, 11/30)? Procurement — can you tentatively confirm whether the amended SOW (small scope change) can be routed through your fast lane? I can prepare a minimal DocuSign in parallel.

I’ll circulate an internal risk snippet for governance in a follow-up.

Thanks,
Camila
Camila Reyes
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
attachments: Acme_MAP_v2.xlsx (spreadsheet, 48KB)


From: Sara Lynn <sara.lynn@acmecorp.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>
Cc: Michael Tan <michael.tan@acmecorp.com>, Procurement Ops <procure@procureco.com>
Date: 2025-11-29T11:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Runway retune & risk notice — Acme MAP schedule drift

Thanks Camila — appreciate the proactive note.

Acme position: we prefer Option A (move start by 10 business days) if Redwood can commit to the 2-week dedicated burst. Our PM (Michael) and I discussed; the integration team needs the extra runway for telemetry fixes. Procurement copied — can you confirm what contract changes that would imply (SOW amendment vs. simple change order)?

We can confirm by EOD tomorrow if we have the burst and the amendment language. Michael, thoughts on acceptance gating?

Best,
Sara Lynn
Director, Platform Integrations
Acme Corp

Internal note / recommended approach:

Summary: Acme wants Option A. I ran the throughput model against current Dedicated capacity and we can cover a 2-week burst, but it requires bumping Acme to a temporary priority queue and a one-time buffer allocation (approx +12% token/instance usage). Risk log highlights attached (risk_log_q4.csv). Key risks:
- Capacity contention if two other Dedicated customers request burst in same 48h window (mitigation: burst backfill policy; preemptible non-critical jobs).
- Increased ops overhead for sequencing releases to avoid KV cache invalidation.
- Contracting: our standard is a minor SOW amendment; legal will need 48h to sign-off.

Recommended steps (short):
1) Confirm capacity burst with Acme and lock start window (target: 2025-12-15 to 2025-12-28).
2) Send procurement a slim SOW amendment draft (Ben will own).
3) Notify infra ops for resource reservation and schedule a dry-run on 12/12.
4) Track risk_log_q4.csv as the live risk registry; escalate to Laura if blockers > 2.

I’m attaching the risk snapshot.

—Ben

Date: Tue, 25 Nov 2025 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Mutual Runbook & Closure Timeline: Launch Window Decision Path

Hi Sana / Tom,

Thanks again for the productive call today — appreciated the clarity on your evaluation criteria. I attached the mutual action plan (MAP_Orchid_v3.xlsx) and an evaluation playbook that maps Redwood deliverables to your acceptance criteria. High-level proposal below so procurement and legal can align on the same timeline.

Key proposed milestones
- Evaluation window: Dec 8–Dec 19 (two-week hands-on trial with weekly checkpoints)
- Final decision (Go/No-Go): Week of Dec 22 (requested sign-off by Dec 23)
- DocuSign signature window: Dec 24–Dec 26 (48–72 hour signer window; DocuSign envelope to be sent after legal confirmation)
- Target production start (soft launch / canary): Jan 19, 2026; full cutover week of Feb 2 if onboarding goes smoothly

Dependencies / asks from Orchid Retail
- Confirm primary signers + availability window for Dec 24–26 (we can shift ±1 week if needed)
- Complete the short security checklist we discussed (SOC 2 scope + data handling note) by Dec 5
- Provide PO template if you require a pre-signed PO before DocuSign

Next steps (from our side)
- Camila will schedule the technical handoff for your evaluation team this week
- Marcus will share a canary runbook and acceptance checklist by Nov 27
- Naomi (legal) will prep the standard SOW + EULA for a quick redline

Attachments: MAP_Orchid_v3.xlsx; Evaluation_Playbook_v1.pdf

If those dates look off, tell me what works and we’ll adapt. Goal is to make the final decision window very predictable for your procurement team.

Best,
Avery
Avery Johnson
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
avery.johnson@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0123

Subject: Re: Mutual Runbook & Closure Timeline: Launch Window Decision Path

Thanks Avery — this is helpful. A few quick items from procurement and security: 

1) Security: our security team needs SOC 2 Type II evidence (or in-progress evidence) and confirmation of audit logs retention (90 days) before we greenlight trial. Can Marcus send the checklist you mentioned to our security alias sec-requests@orchidretail.com?

2) SLA: we need a 99.95% SLO for the production plan. Is that achievable under the soft launch (canary) model, or will that require dedicated capacity?

3) Signature timing: Dec 24–26 is tight for our legal team over the holidays. Would you be able to move the DocuSign window to Jan 6–8 instead? That gives us time for redlines and for procurement to align PO timing. If you prefer to keep the decision by Dec 23, we could conditionally approve pending an exec yes/no and finalize the contract in Jan.

4) PO: attaching our standard PO template — please confirm whether Redwood needs a PO in advance or if DocuSign alone suffices.

We’ll wait for Marcus and Naomi’s notes. If Jan 6–8 is possible for signing, that would be ideal.

Best,
Sana Patel
Head of Platform Ops, Orchid Retail
sana.patel@orchidretail.com

Attachments: PO_Template_Orchid.docx

Hi Sana — thanks for the signal. Quick responses inline: 

- SOC 2 / audit logs: we can provide the SOC 2 in-progress report this week and a public controls map. Audit logs: retention can be configured to meet 90 days in our hosted environment; we’ll include that in the MAP as an acceptance artifact. I’ll send the technical checklist to sec-requests@orchidretail.com by EOD Thu.

- SLA: 99.95% in production normally requires dedicated capacity or a premium routing policy. For the canary/soft-launch (single-region hosted) we can commit to 99.9% SLO with an operational playbook; if you need 99.95% from day one, we’ll need to include a dedicated capacity line item (I’ll add estimated cost to the MAP).

- Signing window: Jan 6–8 is workable from our side. That aligns nicely with your ask and gives Naomi a little more runway for redlines. Let’s aim for a decision path: exec-level conditional yes by Dec 22, then final contract completion during Jan 6–8 signer window.

- Acceptance criteria / canary: proposed approach — 7-day canary for core API traffic (pre-agreed prompt sets and load levels), acceptance metrics tracked in Redwood Console reports. If acceptance passes, schedule full ramp over next two weeks. I’ll attach the canary playbook in a follow-up.

Camila — can you set up the technical onboarding run (two 90-min sessions) the week of Dec 8? Naomi — I’ll loop you on the SOW redline this afternoon.

Thanks,
Marcus
VP, Customer Success, Redwood Inference
marcus.lin@redwood.com

Sana / Tom — great, thanks for the fast alignment. Summary and firm next steps so procurement/legal know the flow: 

Summary decisions
- Security: Redwood will deliver SOC 2 in-progress evidence and the controls map by Nov 28 (Marcus / Naomi).
- SLA: We’ll proceed with canary under 99.9% SLO; Orchid can opt into 99.95% production SLA in the SOW as an add-on with dedicated capacity. Marcus will annotate the MAP with cost delta today.
- Signing window: Agreed Jan 6–8, 2026 for DocuSign. We will treat Dec 22 as an exec-level conditional yes date to keep momentum.
- Start date: Target soft launch (canary) week of Jan 19, 2026; full ramp contingent on canary acceptance (typically 2 weeks).

Action items (owners & ETA)
- Marcus: send technical canary playbook + audit-log config and cost delta for dedicated capacity — by EOD Nov 28.
- Naomi: SOW + SLR for Jan signing round; will share redline-ready doc by Dec 12.
- Camila: schedule two onboarding sessions with Orchid evaluation team for week of Dec 8 — calendar invites to be sent by Nov 30.
- Avery: create DocuSign envelope and pre-populate signers once Naomi confirms final SOW language (target envelope send: Jan 6 at 10:00 PST).

DocuSign & PO flow
- If Orchid can provide a PO ahead of signing, we will accept it as a purchase order on file and proceed to send DocuSign for countersignature during the Jan 6–8 window. Otherwise, DocuSign-only flow is OK.

I’ll attach the final action-oriented MAP after Marcus’s updates: mutual_action_plan_final.xlsx. When Naomi posts the SOW, I’ll create the DocuSign envelope and share the envelope link here.

If there are any blockers on the dates above, call me directly and we’ll triage.

Thanks everyone — moving this to the 'decision path' bucket and will ping again once SOW is in redline form.

Avery

title: Rate card & 50B token/month volume discount inquiry
preview:
From: Jenny Park <jenny.park@acme-analytics.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-11-18T09:12:00-05:00
Subject: Rate card & volume discount for hosted API

Hi Camila,

Were evaluating hosted inference options for a new feature and would like Redwoods official rate card and any guidance on volume discounts. Our initial estimate is ~50B input+output tokens/month (mostly short to medium prompts, average 85 tokens per call).

Specific asks:
- Baseline per-token rate for the hosted API (generation + embeddings usage model).
- Volume tier thresholds and discount percentages for 12-month commit vs month-to-month.
- Any minimum commitment / upfront payment requirements.
- Typical SLA/priority support add-ons and pricing.

Can you share the rate card and do a quick call this week? Were aiming to finalize vendor economics by end of month.

Thanks,
Jenny Park
Head of Platform
Acme Analytics


From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Jenny Park <jenny.park@acme-analytics.com>
Date: 2025-11-18T10:05:00-05:00
Subject: Re: Rate card & volume discount for hosted API

Hi Jenny — thanks for the details and the target volume. Ive attached our Q4 rate card (redwood_ratecard_q4_v2.pdf) and pulled a preliminary note below specific to a 50B token/month profile. Happy to walk through this on a call — would 11/19 10am PT work?

Preliminary (hosted API) economics — ballpark, before any committed discount:
- Baseline: $0.0006 / token for generation model family X (suitable for most short/medium LLM work)
- Embeddings: $0.0004 / token (if used heavily we can package)
- Estimated monthly cost (50B tokens): ~$30,000 / month at baseline

Volume/commit guidance:
- Month-to-month: baseline above (no minimum).
- 12-month committed usage: tiered discounts available by committed monthly volume. For ~50B/month we typically offer a 20–30% committed discount depending on term and paid-in-advance status.
- Additional enterprise add-ons (SLA, dedicated capacity) are priced separately and can be blended into a reserved capacity contract.

Next steps: share a short deck on your usage profile and whether youre looking for a reserved (Dedicated) path or hosted with commit. If youre OK, Ill loop in Ben and Marcus internally to propose final discount/credit structure.

Attachments: redwood_ratecard_q4_v2.pdf (rate card stub)

Best,
Camila Reyes
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference


From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-11-19T08:30:00-08:00
Subject: FW: Rate card & volume discount for hosted API — Acme (50B tokens)

Ben/Marcus — forwarding Acmes ask (50B tokens/month). Theyre leaning hosted/API (not Dedicated) but will consider a 12-month commit. Please see my quick cost math below and advise on discount ladder we can offer without approval headaches. Rachel included for any billing/contract constraints.

Baseline math (hosted, model family X):
- Baseline unit: $0.0006 / token
- Raw cost at baseline: 50,000,000,000 * 0.0006 = $30,000 / month

Proposed tiered committed structure suggestion (12-months):
- 0–5B: baseline (no discount)
- 5–20B: 10% discount
- 20–40B: 20% discount
- 40B+: 28% discount (applies to incremental usage beyond 40B)

For illustrative purposes that puts Acmes blended discount ~24% and monthly spend ~ $22,800. If we want to be more aggressive to win the account (theyre an analytics platform) we could push 30% for a 12-month prepay with a 2–3 month onboarding credit.

Ask: Ben — can you sign off on the 28% top band or recommend limits? Marcus — want to know if sales promotions budget can cover a 30% prepay special. Rachel — any billing/legal flags on a prepay + onboarding credit?

Quoted text below.

> Hi Jenny — thanks for the details and the target volume. Ive attached our Q4 rate card...

Thanks,
Camila

Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-11-19T09:10:00-08:00
Subject: Re: FW: Rate card & volume discount for hosted API — Acme (50B tokens)

- I approve the 28% top-band for incremental >40B for a standard 12-month commit (no prepay).
- We can offer a 30% blended discount if Acme agrees to a 12-month prepay (full amount up front) and we apply a 2-month onboarding credit. That would need Marcus approval for promo budget though.
- Rachel, please confirm there are no payment/billing constraints for a prepay structure and whether we need a special invoice flow.

From a go-to-close perspective, hand this back to Camila to present the two options: (A) 12-month commit, billed monthly, top-band 28%; (B) 12-month prepay, 30% blended + 2-month credit.

Ben


From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Jenny Park <jenny.park@acme-analytics.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: 2025-11-20T15:42:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Rate card & volume discount for hosted API

Hi Jenny — thanks for your patience. Internal alignment complete. Below are two formal options for a 12-month engagement for hosted API consumption at ~50B tokens/month. I attached a draft SOW for the onboarding timeline (Acme_SOW_draft.docx) and included a DocuSign link for the prepay option if you choose that route.

Option A — 12-month commit, billed monthly
- Unit pricing: baseline $0.0006 / token
- Tiered discounts (applied to incremental usage): 0–5B 0%, 5–20B 10%, 20–40B 20%, 40B+ 28%
- Estimated blended discount for 50B: ~24%
- Estimated monthly spend: ~$22,800
- Payment: monthly invoice, standard Net30

Option B — 12-month prepay (single invoice, upfront)
- Unit pricing: same baseline w/ blended 30% discount applied across committed usage
- Onboarding: 2 months credit applied as a consumption credit after go-live
- Estimated effective monthly spend (amortized): ~$21,000
- Payment: single upfront invoice; DocuSign link: https://docusign.example.com/envelope/12345678

SLA/add-ons: priority support and 99.9% uptime SLA available for additional fee — we can provide numbers if youd like to include them in the SOW.

Next steps: if Option A works well send a standard MSA + SOW for signature and set onboarding dates. If Option B works, sign via DocuSign and well apply the credits and begin provisioning immediately. Also happy to hop on a short call to walk the numbers.

Attachments: Acme_SOW_draft.docx, redwood_ratecard_q4_v2.pdf

Best,
Camila Reyes
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference

To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>

Ill review the SOW this afternoon and circle back with any redlines. Can you send the DocuSign envelope and a simple invoice so I can get procurement moving?

Appreciate the quick turnaround — talk soon.

title: Vendor tier reclassification — CloudLink onboarding (DPA & flow-down routing)
preview:
From: Erin Singh <erin.singh@brightlayer.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: BrightLayer Legal <contracts@brightlayer.com>
Date: 2025-11-15T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: Vendor tier change — introducing CloudLink as integration partner / flow-down + DPA

Hi Ben,

Quick heads up: BrightLayer is onboarding CloudLink (managed connectors) to handle part of the telemetry enrichment pipeline for our integration with Redwood. CloudLink will be processing telemetry that may include hashed IDs and low-sensitivity logs. They will be a downstream subprocessor of BrightLayer and we'd like Redwood to accept the usual flow-down and DPA routing so we can proceed with the SOW kickoff.

Attached: CloudLink-SOW.pdf (scope + estimated volumes).

Requests from our side:
- Please confirm Redwood's acceptance of CloudLink as a subprocessor and any required DPA redlines.
- If you need additional evidence (SOC, encryption details, subprocessors list), let us know — CloudLink has a SOC2 summary and can provide a short questionnaire.
- Target kickoff: first week of December; we need contract alignment by Nov 25 to meet that.

Thanks — appreciate a quick turnaround.

Erin Singh
Senior PM, BrightLayer
Erin.Singh@brightlayer.com

--
Attachment: CloudLink-SOW.pdf (PDF, 248 KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>
Cc: Erin Singh <erin.singh@brightlayer.com>, BrightLayer Legal <contracts@brightlayer.com>
Date: 2025-11-15T10:03:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Vendor tier change — introducing CloudLink as integration partner / flow-down + DPA

Team — forwarding BrightLayer's request. TL;DR: BrightLayer wants to add CloudLink as a downstream subprocessor; they attached the SOW and asked us to confirm acceptance and DPA redlines. Timeline: BrightLayer hopes to be contract-aligned by Nov 25.

Specific asks for internal owners:
- Legal (Laura): please review and mark any flow-down redlines we will insist on (audit rights, encryption, breach notification SLA, subcontractor chain).
- Security/Privacy (Rishi): can you confirm if CloudLink's SOC2 summary will be acceptable and whether we need additional controls or a restricted data scope?
- Sales Ops / Procurement (Dev): if we accept with conditions, what's the impact to Order Form / PO wording and billing (SOW addendum)?

Proposed next step: Laura + Rishi give a short decision + required redline list by EOD Monday (2025-11-17), Dev to map to order form changes if we accept. I'll keep BrightLayer in the loop and push for any additional evidence they can provide.

Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com | +1-415-555-0182

--
(Forwarded): CloudLink-SOW.pdf

From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>
Cc: Erin Singh <erin.singh@brightlayer.com>, BrightLayer Legal <contracts@brightlayer.com>
Date: 2025-11-16T08:30:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Vendor tier change — introducing CloudLink as integration partner / flow-down + DPA

Ben / all — reviewed the SOW and drafted initial DPA redlines. High-level comments:

1) Evidence & attestations
- We need CloudLink's SOC2 Type II report or a vendor-signed SOC2 summary and the auditor's letter. The SOC2 summary they sent will be reviewed by Security but does not replace a full report for higher-risk processing.
- If CloudLink cannot share full SOC2, require annual independent audit + right for Redwood (or our auditor) to request evidence via BrightLayer.

2) Flow-down clauses to include (minimum):
- Encryption-in-transit + at-rest specifics (algorithms, key management).
- Defined subprocessors list + 30-day notice for changes.
- Audit & inspection right (scoped and reciprocal).
- Breach notification timing (24-48 hours) and support for investigations.
- Deletion/return of data on termination and proof-of-destruction options.

3) Contract mechanics
- We will accept CloudLink as a subprocessor subject to BrightLayer procuring a DPA that includes the above items or getting CloudLink to sign an exhibit to BrightLayer's DPA.
- We should add an explicit indemnity carve-out for data breaches resulting from CloudLink's negligence.

Attached: redwood-dpa-redlines.docx (track changes). I call out the sections above with suggested language.

Ask from Security: Rishi, please confirm whether the SOC2 summary they referenced is enough for scoped onboarding or if we need the full report + gap remediation.

If everyone is aligned, I'll prepare a short memo for the counterparty (BrightLayer Legal) with redline excerpts and proposed acceptance conditions.

Laura Bennett
Sr. Counsel, Contracts | Redwood Inference
laura_bennett@redwood.com

--
Attachment: redwood-dpa-redlines.docx (DOCX, 47 KB)

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.com>
Cc: Erin Singh <erin.singh@brightlayer.com>
Date: 2025-11-17T11:15:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Vendor tier change — introducing CloudLink as integration partner / flow-down + DPA

Laura — quick summary from Security after a light review of the SOC2 summary:
- CloudLink provided a SOC2 summary dated 2024-09; controls appear standard for SaaS connectors. We have an internal vendor record (see link below) with the auditor name and scope.
- For the data categories described in the SOW (hashed IDs + low-sensitivity logs), the SOC2 summary is probably sufficient for an initial acceptance, provided we restrict the data classes CloudLink may process and require documented encryption key management.

Proposed controls to include in the DPA / flow-down:
- Scope limitation: prohibit processing of unredacted PII and high-risk health/financial data.
- Mandatory encryption at-rest with customer-controlled keys for any persistent storage related to telemetry (if CloudLink persists data longer than 24 hours).
- Quarterly evidence checklist: basic control attestations + access logs for the preceding 90 days on request.

I have attached CloudLink's soc summary we were sent earlier: cloudlink-soc2-summary.pdf. Procurement should ask CloudLink for either the full SOC2 report under NDA or a signed SOC2 bridge letter.

Rishi Malhotra
Head of Security & Privacy | Redwood Inference

From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>

Date: 2025-11-16T08:30:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Vendor tier change — introducing CloudLink as integration partner / flow-down + DPA

Ben / all — reviewed the SOW and drafted initial DPA redlines. High-level comments:

1) Evidence & attestations
- We need CloudLink's SOC2 Type II report or a vendor-signed SOC2 summary and the auditor's letter. The SOC2 summary they sent will be reviewed by Security but does not replace a full report for higher-risk processing.
- If CloudLink cannot share full SOC2, require annual independent audit + right for Redwood (or our auditor) to request evidence via BrightLayer.

2) Flow-down clauses to include (minimum):
- Encryption-in-transit + at-rest specifics (algorithms, key management).
- Defined subprocessors list + 30-day notice for changes.
- Audit & inspection right (scoped and reciprocal).
- Breach notification timing (24-48 hours) and support for investigations.
- Deletion/return of data on termination and proof-of-destruction options.

3) Contract mechanics
- We will accept CloudLink as a subprocessor subject to BrightLayer procuring a DPA that includes the above items or getting CloudLink to sign an exhibit to BrightLayer's DPA.
- We should add an explicit indemnity carve-out for data breaches resulting from CloudLink's negligence.

Attached: redwood-dpa-redlines.docx (track changes). I call out the sections above with suggested language.

Ask from Security: Rishi, please confirm whether the SOC2 summary they referenced is enough for scoped onboarding or if we need the full report + gap remediation.

If everyone is aligned, I'll prepare a short memo for the counterparty (BrightLayer Legal) with redline excerpts and proposed acceptance conditions.

Laura Bennett
Sr. Counsel, Contracts | Redwood Inference
laura_bennett@redwood.com

Date: 2025-11-17T11:15:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Vendor tier change — introducing CloudLink as integration partner / flow-down + DPA

Laura — quick summary from Security after a light review of the SOC2 summary:
- CloudLink provided a SOC2 summary dated 2024-09; controls appear standard for SaaS connectors. We have an internal vendor record (see link below) with the auditor name and scope.
- For the data categories described in the SOW (hashed IDs + low-sensitivity logs), the SOC2 summary is probably sufficient for an initial acceptance, provided we restrict the data classes CloudLink may process and require documented encryption key management.

Proposed controls to include in the DPA / flow-down:
- Scope limitation: prohibit processing of unredacted PII and high-risk health/financial data.
- Mandatory encryption at-rest with customer-controlled keys for any persistent storage related to telemetry (if CloudLink persists data longer than 24 hours).
- Quarterly evidence checklist: basic control attestations + access logs for the preceding 90 days on request.

I have attached CloudLink's soc summary we were sent earlier: cloudlink-soc2-summary.pdf. Procurement should ask CloudLink for either the full SOC2 report under NDA or a signed SOC2 bridge letter.

title: Advance credit drawdown workflow & VAT handling for DataStream
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Laura Cheng <laura.cheng@datastream.com>, DataStream Procurement <procurement@datastream.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 18 Nov 2025 08:15:00 +0000
Subject: Advance credit drawdown workflow & VAT handling for DataStream

Hi Laura / team,

Thanks again for confirming the committed annual spend. Attached is a proposed operational workflow for how Redwood will issue invoices and apply drawdowns against the advance credit: the sheet lists an initial prepayment invoice (covering the committed amount), then monthly drawdown invoices that net against the credit balance. Key points: 

- Invoice cadence: Redwood issues one prepay invoice up front, then issues monthly drawdown statements that apply the credit balance; invoices will show the gross usage, the credit applied, and the net amount due (typically zero while credit remains).
- VAT/tax posture: We need your VAT registration country and VAT ID to determine whether the reverse-charge applies. If DataStream provides an EU VAT ID, we will include a reverse-charge note and zero-rate VAT on customer invoices where applicable. Otherwise standard VAT will be charged.
- PO & billing entity: please confirm the legal billing entity and PO number to appear on invoices.
- Reporting: included is a simple Drawdown Ledger CSV that we'll update monthly and share to your billing contact.

Attachments: [Attachment: 2025-Advance-Credit-Schedule-DS-0042.xlsx] [Attachment: Drawdown-Ledger-Initial.csv]

Next steps: Can you confirm VAT ID and the preferred cadence (monthly vs. quarterly drawdown statements)? If monthly is fine we'll start drawdowns on Dec 1st against the prepay.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0132

---
Redwood Inference | Developer-first inference platform

3) PO number — we'll need a PO on each invoice. Can you confirm invoicing will include the PO line and also the contract reference (DS-2025-0042)?

title: Re: Pre-launch attribution & logo use — notice period and redlines
preview:
From: Emma Lin <emma@sunriseco.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 12 Nov 2025 09:15:00 -0800
Subject: Order form redlines — publicity/press notice questions

Hi Aisha (and Ben),

Thank you again for the demo yesterday — excited about getting SunriseCo into the pilot. Our legal team attached the first-pass redlines to the Order Form and flagged the publicity section (MSA/Order Form references) as a key blocker for our marketing team. High-level asks: a) a defined notice period before either party issues a public announcement; b) clear constraints on logo usage (color/grid, co-branding); and c) an option to anonymize quotes/case studies for early-stage pilots.

I've attached the redlined Order Form and a short note from our legal counsel. Can Redwood propose specific language for a 1) notice window and 2) logo usage terms? We'd like to close the contract this month.

Attachments: Sunrise_OrderForm_redlines_v1.docx (docx), Sunrise_legal_note.pdf (pdf)

Thanks,
Emma Lin
Head of Partnerships | SunriseCo
emma@sunriseco.com

---


From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.ai>
To: Emma Lin <emma@sunriseco.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 12 Nov 2025 10:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Order form redlines — publicity/press notice questions

Emma — thanks for sending. I’ve looped in Ben (sales) and Marissa (legal) to propose concrete language. Goal: keep the ask narrow so procurement can sign quickly.

Quick context for us: we typically ask for a short notice window to review co-ordinated announcements and a logo usage spec (we provide a brand kit and prefer a single approved asset). For pilots we also offer an anonymized case study option. 

Marissa / Ben — can you suggest redline language for: 1) pre-announcement notice (we usually prefer 7 business days but are flexible), 2) allowed logo treatments (color vs grayscale, placement limits), and 3) whether an anonymized quote option satisfies Sunrise? Emma — we'll aim to get a suggested redline back by EOD tomorrow.

Aisha Rahman
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
aisha.rahman@redwood.ai
(415) 555-0198

---


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.ai>, Emma Lin <emma@sunriseco.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 13 Nov 2025 11:02:00 -0800
Subject: Suggested commercial compromise (notice + logo)

Hi Emma / Aisha / Marissa — taking a commercial-first approach here. Proposed language for the Order Form publicity section (high level):

- Announcement notice: "Each party shall provide the other party with at least seven (7) business days' prior written notice before issuing any public press release or announcement referencing the other party's name, trademarks, or logos."

- Logo use: "SunriseCo grants Redwood a non-exclusive, revocable right to use SunriseCo's name and logo solely to identify SunriseCo as a customer. Redwood shall use only the provided master logo files and will not modify colors, proportions, or incorporate the logo into other marks without prior written consent. Grayscale versions for press and tight-space placement are permitted with written brand guidelines provided by SunriseCo."

- Case studies/quotes: "For pilot engagements, SunriseCo may opt for anonymized case studies; if SunriseCo chooses to be named, Redwood will route final copy for a five (5) business day approval prior to publication."

I've attached a short doc with the suggested clause text tracked into the Order Form. I'm proposing 7 business days (Ben prefers this for speed to sign) but can move to 10 if legal requires.

Attachment: Redwood_publicity_clause_suggestion.docx

Cheers,
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Manager | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai

---


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.ai>, Emma Lin <emma@sunriseco.com>
Date: Fri, 14 Nov 2025 09:45:00 -0800
Subject: RE: Suggested commercial compromise (notice + logo)

Thanks Ben — I reviewed the suggested wording and have two legal-focused edits to propose (keeps the commercial intent but reduces ambiguity):

1) Change "prior written notice" to "prior written notice reasonably sufficient to permit review" — this gives us flexibility for urgent joint announcements while preserving Sunrise's ability to review. If Sunrise wants a strict minimum, propose "no less than five (5) business days' prior written notice".

2) For logo use, add a sentence: "Redwood shall not use SunriseCo's logo in paid advertising or third-party co-marketing without prior written consent."

3) For case studies, replace the five (5) business day approval with "a single approval cycle not to exceed five (5) business days from receipt of final copy; failure to respond shall be deemed approval." Helps avoid repeated rounds.

I will attach a tracked version of the Order Form with these edits. If Sunrise prefers a definitive minimum for the announcement window, I recommend five (5) business days to balance marketing needs and review capacity.

Attachment: Sunrise_OrderForm_redlines_v1_edit_mco.docx

Best,
Marissa Cole
Senior Counsel, Commercial Contracts | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai

---

Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>

Subject: Proposed final compromise for quick signature

Emma — thanks for patience. To expedite, here's the compromise we're prepared to accept into the Order Form: 

- Announcement notice: Five (5) business days' prior written notice. (Marissa's recommended minimum.)
- Logo use: Redwood may use provided master logo files and grayscale variants for press and case studies; Redwood will not use the logo in paid advertising or third-party co-marketing without prior written consent.
- Case studies/quotes: Anonymized case study option available; if Sunrise elects to be named, Redwood will route final copy for a single approval cycle not to exceed five (5) business days, after which approval will be deemed granted.

If Sunrise confirms this is acceptable, we'll produce a final tracked Order Form and share a DocuSign link for signature. Quick ask: can Sunrise provide their high-res logo and brand guidelines (preferred file formats and color hex) so marketing can pre-stage assets? If you send the package we can attach it to the contract for reference.

I’ve included a zip with Sunrise logo specs you shared earlier — I’ll exchange for the high-res master files when you confirm.

Thanks — looking forward to closing this week.
Ben

title: Request: activity provenance packet and export validators
preview:
From: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@novamed.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>
Cc: security@novamed.com
Date: Thu, 13 Nov 2025 09:22:00 -0800
Subject: Request: sample activity provenance packet and export validators

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the quick intro this morning. As part of our SIG+SOC2 evidence collection we need two things clarified before we schedule a hands-on review: a concise inventory of what "admin/activity" events you persist and an example export we can use to validate our ingestion pipeline. Our audit team is specifically asking: 

- Which event types are recorded (e.g., user login, API key use, model invocation, KV cache operations, policy/role changes, config updates, escalation handoffs)?
- For each event type: stored attributes (actor, actor_id, ip, user_agent, request_id, tokens_consumed, model_id, before/after snapshots, cluster_node_id, region) and whether payloads contain user-generated content or PII.
- Default retention for admin activity vs. operator events, and whether per-tenant retention overrides are possible.
- Export formats supported (JSONL, Parquet, compressed tarball), timezone normalization, and manifest/checksum strategy.
- Redaction options we can assert (automated PII scrubbing, field-level tokenization, and available redaction reports).

Could you send a small sample export (time window ~24h, obfuscated PII) and a short note about retention and on-demand extraction steps? Ideally we can validate ingest by Monday morning (Nov 17). We will treat the sample as controlled — no production access will be attempted.

Thanks,
Lena Ortiz
Senior Information Security Manager
Novamed Therapeutics

---
Attachment: request_template_for_exports.csv (csv)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>
To: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@novamed.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.inference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.inference.com>
Date: Thu, 13 Nov 2025 10:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: sample activity provenance packet and export validators

Lena — thanks for the clear list. I'm pulling together a small obfuscated sample and a one-page inventory. Before I package, two quick clarifications so we give exactly what you need: 

1) When you say "operator events" do you want privileged session recordings (console commands) included, or only audit-row events (create/update/delete actions)?
2) For the ingestion test: do you want JSONL with a separate CSV manifest, or Parquet + manifest? JSONL + CSV manifest is easiest for us to render quickly.

Plan: Kimberly will add the event-to-field mapping and retention matrix; Jordan will generate a 24h JSONL slice with PII obfuscated and checksums. I'll deliver the package and a short playbook with the extraction steps and the Drive link for download by EOD today.

Best,
Ben
Director, Solutions — Redwood Inference

---
[Ben's calendar link: https://cal.redwood.inference/ben/15m]

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.inference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.inference.com>
Date: Thu, 13 Nov 2025 11:18:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: sample activity provenance packet and export validators

Ben / Jordan — sending the field mapping and retention summary below (short form). I'll attach the formal matrix to the package.

Event classes we persist (high level):
- Auth events: user_login, api_token_create, api_token_revoke, mfa_change
- Access control: role_assignment, policy_change, group_membership_change
- Operator/admin actions: config_update, deployment_rollout, model_pin, model_unpin, canary_trigger
- Invocation-level metadata: model_invoke (per-request metadata only — not full user prompt unless customer opts into logging), embedding_create, batch_rerank
- System/infra events: node_reboot, gpu_preempt, kv_cache_eviction

Common stored attributes per-row: timestamp_utc, event_type, actor_name, actor_id, actor_role, tenant_id, request_id, src_ip, geo_country, user_agent, object_type, object_id, change_summary (diff if applicable), model_id (if invocation), token_count, sequence_length, cluster_zone, checksum_sha256.

Retention policy (defaults):
- Auth & Access control: 24 months
- Operator/Admin action rows: 36 months
- Invocation-level metadata (non prompt): 12 months
- Full prompt logs (if enabled by customer): configurable, default 30 days

Export formats we support for evidence handoff: JSONL (newline-delimited), Parquet (columnar), compressed tar.gz of either, plus a CSV manifest with checksums and a small attestation JSON (signed by our export service). Timezones are normalized to UTC in exports; timestamps include original offset where available. We compute SHA256 checksums for each file and a top-level manifest (manifest.csv) with file size and SHA256.

Redaction options: SHA-1 truncation for identifiers, deterministic tokenization for emails (hash+domain-preserve), and full-string redaction for free-text prompts. We generate a redaction_report.csv that lists which rows/fields were redacted and why.

Notes on PII: by default our admin activity rows do not include user-submitted content; invocation prompt bodies are stored only if customer toggles verbose logging. For Novamed's sample we'll obfuscate tenant IDs and replace emails with hashed placeholders.

I'll attach: redaction_matrix_v2.xlsx and the mapping CSV. Jordan, can you produce the JSONL slice with schema-compliant rows and place in Drive when ready?

Kimberly Park
Sr. Compliance Engineer — Redwood Inference

Attachment: redaction_matrix_v2.xlsx (xlsx)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>
To: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@novamed.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.inference.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.inference.com>
Date: Fri, 14 Nov 2025 16:03:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request: sample activity provenance packet and export validators

Hi Lena — attaching the sample package and the short export playbook. Summary below and link to download: 

What we sent:
- sample_admin_slice.jsonl (24h obfuscated slice; 500 rows covering auth, admin_action, model_invoke, infra_event)
- export_manifest_template.csv (manifest with checksums and file sizes)
- redaction_matrix_v2.xlsx (fields we redacted and why)
- small README: extraction_steps.txt (one-page list showing how to request an on-demand export via the Console API and our SFTP fallback)

Key points for your ingest test:
- Files are in UTC; timestamps are ISO-8601.
- Each JSON row has a request_id you can use to correlate across files; request_id is stable across related events.
- We included a manifest.csv with per-file SHA256 and a top-level attestation.json signed by our export process.
- Default retention limits are documented in the attached retention_matrix. If you need a longer retention carveout we can propose a contract amendment or isolated dedicated retention for the tenant.

Download: https://drive.google.com/drive/fake/Novamed-AdminProvenance-Sample (link expires in 7 days)

If you'd like to walk through the sample tomorrow morning we can do a 30m session — propose Nov 15 9:30am PST or Nov 16 10:00am PST.

Thanks — Ben
Director, Solutions — Redwood Inference

---

> Ben / Jordan — sending the field mapping and retention summary below (short form). I'll attach the formal matrix to the package.
>
> [redaction and export detail omitted in-line — see attached redaction_matrix_v2.xlsx]
>
> Kimberly
>
Attachments: sample_admin_slice.jsonl (jsonl), export_manifest_template.csv (csv), redaction_matrix_v2.xlsx (xlsx)
